subject: [PATCH v4 3/4] cleanup: Annotate guard constructors with __nonnull()

reviews.author: Peter Zijlstra
reviews.summary: Reviewer Peter Zijlstra noted that the build robot reported an issue with clang-23 being confused by the patch, and suggested that Dmitry Ilvokhin is likely CC'd on the email.
reviews.raw_body:
The build robot found something to hate in this one. I think you're on
Cc there. It looks to me like clang-23 is confused somehow, but who
knows.

2026-05-23.patch_summary: This patch annotates guard constructors in the Linux kernel with __nonnull(), which tells the compiler to warn when a NULL pointer is statically known to be passed. This is done by re-declaring the constructor with __nonnull() for DEFINE_GUARD() and directly annotating it for __DEFINE_LOCK_GUARD_1(). The __nonnull() macro is defined in compiler_attributes.h, following existing conventions. This change aims to improve code quality by catching potential NULL pointer issues at compile-time.

reviews.author: Dmitry Ilvokhin
reviews.summary: Identified a conflict between the __nonnull() macro and glibc's own __nonnull macro. Proposed three options to resolve the issue: dropping the macro, renaming it, or defining it as an alias.

reviews.author: Miguel Ojeda
reviews.summary: Expressed concern about the naming convention and suggested exploring alternative spellings, such as 'nonnull' or '__knonnull', to establish a standard.
reviews.raw_body:
On Tue, May 26, 2026 at 5:13 PM Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com> wrote:
>
> They usually don't collide, except for User Mode Linux builds, which
> include both kernel and userspace headers.

:(

What about other similar names? i.e. a variation of your option 2,
e.g. just `nonnull` (we also have others like that, i.e. no
underscore, e.g. `noinline`), or `___nonnull` (triple underscore, but
may be confusing), or a suffix/prefix letter, e.g. `__knonnull` (for
kernel nonnull)...

i.e. it would be nice to have a "standard" spelling for ourselves, and
also replace the existing `__attribute__((nonnull))`s we have
elsewhere in the tree.

Cheers,
Miguel

2026-05-26.patch_summary: The patch aims to annotate guard constructors with __nonnull() to improve code clarity and consistency.
